lessons.title: Intro to AI (Plain Language)
lessons.summary: What AI is, what it can and cannot do, and how it can help you.

steps.text: AI is like a helpful assistant on your device. It follows instructions to help with writing, answering questions, and organizing.
practicePrompts: What are three things you can help me with today?

steps.text: AI does not have feelings. It gives answers based on patterns in information.
You are in charge. You can ask AI to slow down, explain in simple words, or repeat steps.
Safety first: never share bank details, passwords, or ID numbers in chats.
steps.safetyTip: Never share personal or financial information unless you started the conversation and you trust the website.

keyPoints: AI is a tool that follows instructions.
Ask for simple explanations and repeats.
tryNow: What can you help me do today?

lessons.title: How to Make Video Calls
lessons.summary: Find a video calling app and call a friend.

steps.text: Open your video call app (FaceTime, WhatsApp, Zoom, or similar).
steps.safetyTip: Only accept calls from people you know.

steps.text: Find your contact's name. Tap it to open their details.
Tap the video camera button to start a video call.
If the picture or sound is poor, move closer to your Wi‑Fi and speak clearly.

keyPoints: Open your preferred app and find the person.
Tap the camera icon to start a video call.
For problems, try Wi‑Fi and speak clearly.
tryNow: Call a family member for a 1‑minute test call.

lessons.title: Writing Holiday Letters with AI
lessons.summary: Draft a warm holiday letter with AI's help.

steps.text: Tell the AI who the letter is for and the occasion.
practicePrompts: Write a warm holiday letter to my neighbors, Pat and Alex.
steps.safetyTip: Do not share addresses or private info.

steps.text: Add a few notes: highlights of your year, family news, and a friendly wish.
practicePrompts: Include that I visited my sister in June and started a gardening club.

steps.text: Ask the AI to shorten or simplify the letter if you like.
practicePrompts: Make it 6 sentences in plain language.

steps.text: Copy the letter into your email or print it to mail.

keyPoints: Say who it is for and the occasion.
tryNow: Write a friendly two‑paragraph holiday note to my bridge group.

lessons.title: Organizing Photos
lessons.summary: Find, name, and organize photos on your device.

steps.text: Open your Photos app.
Create a new album named 'Family' or 'Trips'.
Select photos and add them to the album.
Search for 'dog', 'beach', or a month to quickly find pictures.

keyPoints: Add photos to the right album.
tryNow: Make a 'Family' album and add 5 photos.

steps.text: Open your Reminders or Calendar app.
Add a new reminder: 'Doctor on Tuesday at 2 PM'.
Set an alert 1 day before and 1 hour before.
Check today's list each morning.

keyPoints: Use alerts so you get notified.
tryNow: Add 'Take a 10‑minute walk at 4 PM' with an alert.

lessons.title: Spotting Scams & Staying Safe
lessons.summary: Simple rules to keep you safe online.

steps.text: Unexpected messages asking for money are likely scams.
steps.safetyTip: Never send money or gift cards from a message or call you did not start.

steps.text: Do not share codes sent by text or email with anyone.
Check the sender: official emails end with company names (like @bankname.com).
When unsure, ask a trusted family member before clicking links.

keyPoints: If it feels rushed or threatening, stop.
Ask family before clicking unusual links.
tryNow: Show me a checklist to spot scam emails.

lessons.title: Fun with AI (Recipes, Trivia, Hobbies)
lessons.summary: Enjoy safe and simple activities with AI.

steps.text: Ask for a simple recipe using what's in your fridge.
practicePrompts: Give me a simple dinner using chicken, carrots, and rice.

steps.text: Try trivia or a joke for fun.
practicePrompts: Tell me a light, family‑friendly joke.

steps.text: Ask for hobby tips: gardening, crafts, or music.

keyPoints: Use AI for ideas and fun.
tryNow: Plan a 20‑minute fun activity with simple steps.

lessons.title: Using Voice to Talk to Your Device
lessons.summary: Turn on dictation and speak instead of typing.

steps.text: Find the microphone button on your keyboard or app.
Tap it and speak slowly and clearly.
Say 'period' or 'comma' to add punctuation.

tryNow: Dictate a short note to yourself.
